mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold, invalidating the negative predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns in over 60% of observed cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during late-cycle expansions signal manufacturing weakness that rapidly translates into negative short-term equity returns due to profit margin compression.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive 5-day forward returns in late-cycle regimes, or the mechanism shows no directional edge compared to early-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 2.5% trigger expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5, demonstrating the threshold fails to predict negative price action.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue projections, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows, or Rank IC stays below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP combined with falling unemployment rates confirms economic acceleration that boosts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising GDPC1 and falling UNRATE yields negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during monetary tightening regimes indicates credit-constrained manufacturing that suppresses earnings growth and causes negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 60-day returns in over 60% of instances, or the regime-specific signal loses predictive power in walk-forward tests.
